name: Leal, Rebecca
evidence: No public profile could be corroborated for a Rebecca Leal in the 78737 (southwest Austin / Dripping Springs area) zip working as a self-employed consultant. LinkedIn results for the name resolve to a teacher and a hospitality housekeeper, neither matching the occupation or zip; no bio page, firm page, news mention, TEC lobbyist entry, or PAC-linked FEC record was found. Classification rests solely on the self-reported occupation/employer on the filing.

name: Suddaby, Benjamin
resolved_employer: Travis County — Purchasing Operations Specialist II
evidence: Benjamin Cornelio Suddaby is an Operations Specialist II in Travis County Purchasing (county staff directory lists benjamin.suddaby@traviscountytx.gov; public payroll records show the same title), matching the filing's occupation "Operations Specialist" and employer "Travis County". He is also a longtime AFSCME Local 1624 activist who served as Local 1624 president (2023-2024) and is a Local 1624 delegate to the Austin Central Labor Council; the heavy Democratic federal giving pattern (178 contributions) is consistent with a union officer. No TEC lobbyist registration and no ideological-PAC contributions surfaced.

affiliations.snippet: Austin Monitor coverage identifies Ben Suddaby as "an operations specialist for Travis County" and president of Local 1624, who with business manager Carol Guthrie asked interim City Manager Jesus Garza to postpone telework changes.
Local 1624's site lists its Austin CLC delegates, including "Ben Suddaby," among 16 named delegates.

Greg Casar from January 2023 until March 2026, and earlier as senior advisor to Austin Mayor Steve Adler, policy advisor/counsel to then-Council Member Casar, and a fellow and staff attorney at Texas RioGrande Legal Aid. The filed employer 'Self' is consistent with her March 2026 departure from congressional staff, and the Austin location, attorney occupation, and heavily Democratic federal giving pattern all corroborate identity. No affiliation with pro-Israel, liberal-Zionist, or pro-Palestine advocacy organizations, oil & gas, gun-rights, gun-control, or defense-contractor entities was found in public records; her itemized federal PAC recipients could not be enumerated directly because the FEC data API was rate-limited during research.

name: Avery Tabrizi, Dona
resolved_employer: None (reports unemployed); household holds Austin residential rental property entities (Tabrizi and Co Inc / Tabrizi Investments LLC) registered at her home address
evidence: Dona Avery Tabrizi of 1102 Claire Ave, Austin TX 78703 (matches filing zip); named plaintiff with spouse Ali Tabrizi in Tabrizi v. City of Austin (Tex. App. 2018), a land-use suit over a building permit on a .56-acre remainder lot the couple bought in 2013, litigated against the City's Planning/Zoning, Development Services and Environmental officers. Public records list Tabrizi and Co Inc and Tabrizi Investments LLC (NAICS: lessors of residential buildings and dwellings) at the same address. Spouse Ali Tabrizi is an Austin angel/VC investor (CTAN, LiveOak, Capital Factory). She reports her own occupation as unemployed, so she is classified Not Employed; the real-estate and land-use exposure is household-level, not a personal employer.

affiliations.org: Tabrizi v. City of Austin (land-use litigation)
affiliations.snippet: Ali and Dona Tabrizi v. City of Austin; Greg Guernsey, Director of Planning and Zoning; J. Rodney Gonzales, Development Services; Charles Lesniak, Environmental Officer — dispute over the Tabrizis' effort to build on an undeveloped .56-acre lot purchased in 2013.

name: Lorenz, Sheridan
resolved_employer: Mitchell Family Corporation (The Woodlands, TX) — the Mitchell family holding company/family office; past vice president
evidence: Sheridan Mitchell Lorenz, Austin TX 78703 — corroborated by FEC individual-contribution records listing 'Sheridan Lorenz, Austin TX 78703, employer Mitchell Family Corp.' She is a daughter of Cynthia and George P. Mitchell (Mitchell Energy & Development; credited with pioneering commercial shale-gas extraction), past vice president of the Mitchell Family Corporation and of Mitchell Historic Properties (Galveston real estate), president/director of G-1 Corporation, VP and managing member of Circle Ten Inc., and a director of Mitchell Island Investments. She is a board member of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation, which funds climate-change, renewable-energy, aquifer-protection and land-conservation work. Her personal wealth derives from an oil-and-gas fortune, but no public record shows her holding an executive, board, or ownership role in an operating oil & gas company, so no oil_gas flag is recorded.

affiliations.snippet: Sheridan Lorenz is a past vice president of both the Mitchell Family Corporation and Mitchell Historic Properties, and a daughter of George and Cynthia Woods Mitchell.

FEC-reported federal giving from Austin 78703 includes ActBlue, End Citizens United, Senate Majority PAC (SMP), Wendy Davis for Congress and Shannon Hutcheson for Congress — no pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, oil & gas, gun-rights, gun-control or defense-contractor PACs found.

name: Puzycki, Ryan
resolved_employer: Freelance writer; author of the 'City of Yes' urbanism newsletter; fellow, Roots of Progress Institute
evidence: Ryan Puzycki of Austin (78702) is a writer and housing advocate who publishes 'City of Yes,' a weekly urbanism/housing/public-policy newsletter, and is a fellow of the Roots of Progress Institute — matching the filing's 'Writer / Freelance.' He is a serving City of Austin Zoning and Platting Commissioner (District 7 appointee, secretary; second term May 2025–Feb 2027), a board member of AURA (Austin's grassroots urbanist organization), and sits on the Austin Monitor's Strategic Advisory Council. Career background: equity research analyst on Wall Street, then a decade building and managing Montessori schools. Notable for transparency purposes: he is a sitting city land-use commissioner donating in a council race.

affiliations.org: City of Austin Zoning and Platting Commission
affiliations.snippet: Ryan Puzycki serves as secretary of the Austin Zoning and Platting Commission representing District 7, appointed to a second term running May 8, 2025 to Feb 28, 2027.

In Austin he sits on the Austin Monitor's Strategic Advisory Council and the board of AURA, Austin's grassroots urbanist organization, and has been active in the local and state housing reform movement.
Listed as a Roots of Progress Institute fellow; writes on urbanism, housing and public policy from a progress/abundance perspective.

name: Harris, Andrew
resolved_employer: inKind (Austin, TX) — co-founder and Chief Risk Officer; also an owner/investor in Austin restaurants
evidence: Andrew Harris is a co-founder and Chief Risk Officer of inKind, the Austin-based restaurant-capital company (previously EquityEats; provides restaurants funding by pre-purchasing food-and-beverage credit resold via a consumer app), matching the filing's 'Risk officer / InKind.' He was previously inKind's COO and its compliance officer at EquityEats, and before that an employment lawyer at Linklaters, with earlier stints at RBS and a legal fellowship at Immigration Equality; educated at the London School of Economics. He has invested in 30+ restaurants and owned several, including The Guest House and Ember Kitchen in Austin. Caveat: 'Andrew Harris' is a common name, so the 206 aggregate prior FEC donations attributed to this donor record may include other individuals; no ideological PAC giving could be confirmed for this specific person.

affiliations.role: Co-founder and Chief Risk Officer (formerly COO)
affiliations.snippet: Co-founders Johann Moonesinghe and Andrew Harris built inKind after their own experience as restaurant owners and investors; Harris serves as chief risk officer and has invested in over 30 restaurants, owning several including The Guest House and Ember Kitchen in Austin.

name: Snyder, Rodney
resolved_employer: Junction Strategies, LLC — Principal and Founder (agriculture, climate and environmental policy consulting)
evidence: Rod (Rodney) Snyder founded Junction Strategies, LLC in November 2024, a consulting firm on agriculture, climate and environmental policy; he lives on his family farm in Shenandoah Junction, WV, immediately adjacent to Shepherdstown (zip 25443) — matching the filing's employer, occupation 'Consultant' and location. He served in the Biden-Harris Administration 2021-2024 as Senior Advisor for Agriculture to the EPA Administrator and as the first director of EPA's Office of Agriculture and Rural Affairs; previously President of Field to Market (2014-2021), Public Policy Director at the National Corn Growers Association, and Government Affairs Leader at CropLife America. FEC records show Rodney/Rod Snyder (Field to Market / Consultant, WV) giving to ActBlue, the Committee to Elect Richard Ojeda and Talley Sergent for West Virginia in 2018 — Democratic committees only; no PAC giving found on either side of the Israel/Palestine spectrum, no oil & gas, firearms, or defense-contractor PAC records, and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration.

Junction Strategies' About page lists Rod Snyder as Vice Chair of the West Virginia Democratic Party, 2020-2021.
Sand County Foundation announced that Rod Snyder, principal and founder of Junction Strategies and former EPA Senior Advisor for Agriculture, joined its board of directors.
His firm bio lists board memberships at the Sand County Foundation and the Cultivating Change Foundation.

affiliations.role: Chair of the Pesticide Policy Coalition; formerly Government Affairs Leader at CropLife America (pesticide industry trade association)
affiliations.snippet: Coverage of his EPA appointment notes he held roles at the National Corn Growers Association and CropLife America and served as chair of the Pesticide Policy Coalition and on the board of the Conservation Technology Information Center.
